title: Strict Roman to Integer
description:
Convert a Roman numeral string to an integer using strict validation.

Rules:
- Symbols: I=1, V=5, X=10, L=50, C=100, D=500, M=1000.
- Input must be uppercase and represent a value between 1 and 3999 inclusive.
- Valid subtractive pairs are only IV, IX, XL, XC, CD, and CM.
- I may be subtracted from V and X only; X from L and C only; C from D and M only.
- V, L, and D are never repeated.
- I, X, C, and M may appear at most three times consecutively.

Raise ValueError for any invalid input (empty string, lowercase letters, illegal subtractive notation, repeated symbols, out-of-range values, or unknown characters).
